stories.headline: Kyler Murray Rides North: Vikings Stake a Dollar Claim on a Fallen Star
stories.read: Well pardner, Arizona cut Kyler Murray loose and Minnesota lassoed him for the league minimum, with the Cardinals payin' near all the freight. Now he's in a straight-up showdown with J.J. McCarthy for the reins of Kevin O'Connell's wagon, the same trail boss who squeezed gold out of Darnold and Cousins before him. Whoever wins the saddle still gets to feed Justin Jefferson, so that big hoss rides steady either way. Murray at a rock-bottom price is cheap gold if he takes the job, but keep your powder dry on both quarterbacks 'til the camp dust settles.

stories.headline: Aiyuk Fires on His Own Rescue Party
stories.read: Brandon Aiyuk spent the holiday weekend takin' potshots at Jayden Daniels on the socials, which is a mighty strange way to court the one outfit fixin' to take him in. He's still parked on the reserve list out in San Francisco, and word from the wire is Washington would only offer a cheap prove-it deal if the Niners ever turn him loose, with no trade comin' at all. Until that man petitions his way back onto a field, he's a dry well with a famous name on it. Let some other fool in your outfit pay for the memory.

stories.headline: Luther Burden III: The Vein Nobody's Struck in Chicago
stories.read: The Bears shipped D.J. Moore off to Buffalo, and that leaves near 150 throws sittin' unclaimed on the table with young Luther Burden first in line for the haul. As a rookie he wrung more out of every route he ran than any man in the league save Puka Nacua and Smith-Njigba, and head man Ben Johnson's already hollerin' that he's buyin' stock. Meanwhile the saloon's lettin' him walk as the 28th receiver in the whole land grab. That's a vein nobody's struck yet, pardner. Ride in three rounds early and tip your hat to ol' Doc come December.
